assertions.reason: The flag `--disable-todo-continuation` appears in both the dev-wrapper help capture and the built `dist/cli.js` help capture, so the shared CLI help surface exposes the flag as required.

A `--print` run created two pending todos via `todowrite` and exited single-shot with final text `saved`; the JSON print-mode event stream shows the `todowrite` tool result, then a final assistant `saved`, with no extra user follow-up message and no `SANEPI` directive.
`./pi-test.sh --disable-todo-continuation --help` completed successfully and still printed the flag, showing the dev wrapper passes it through to the CLI help surface.

frictions: The JSON print-mode event stream is verbose; targeted searches were needed to confirm the `todowrite` result, the final assistant `saved`, and the absence of any follow-up user directive.

commands.observation: The help output completed successfully and listed `--disable-todo-continuation`.
The help output still listed `--disable-todo-continuation`, confirming wrapper flag pass-through.
The built CLI help completed successfully and listed `--disable-todo-continuation`.

commands.command: ./pi-test.sh --no-session --print "Create and persist a todo list using the todowrite tool with exactly two incomplete items, then reply only with the single word saved."
commands.observation: The single-shot print run exited 0 and emitted only `saved`.

commands.command: ./pi-test.sh --no-session --mode json --print "Create and persist a todo list using the todowrite tool with exactly two incomplete items, then reply only with the single word saved."
commands.observation: The JSON event stream showed a `todowrite` tool result with two pending todos, then a final assistant `saved`, with no extra user follow-up or `SANEPI` directive before `agent_end`.

summary: Validated 3 manual-cli assertions (VAL-CROSS-007, VAL-CROSS-020, VAL-CROSS-021); all 3 passed.
